hypothesis: 200d regime gate + time-series vol targeting on top of 12-1 momentum lifts OOS Sharpe without added decay
notes: REJECT on watchlist: loses to baseline OOS (-0.751 vs -0.592) AND worse decay (0.487 vs 0.395). MEASUREMENT CAVEAT: both candidate and baseline under-deployed on the 12-name book (turnover ~0.6-1.7%, net -6 to -12% over a tech bull mkt) -> ATR risk-sizing barely invests; watchlist walk-forward is measurement-tainted. CONTRAST: on diversified ETF universe the overlay WON OOS (+0.32 vs -0.63, legacy row). Universe-dependent. fitness uses turnover=0 (not captured per-strategy). Single-split only; needs deployment fix + multi-split + deflated-Sharpe before any trust.

hypothesis: Switching sizing from risk_based to vol_target (exposure lever) fixes the ~15% under-deployment and lifts OOS Sharpe on the watchlist
notes: DIAGNOSTIC WIN, OOS REJECT. vol_target sizing fixed deployment WHEN names are held: IS exposure 15%->80%, IS Sharpe +0.47, IS CAGR +13.5%. BUT OOS exposure still ~14% because the bottleneck is SELECTION not sizing: positive-12-1-momentum empties the book in the 2022 bear and re-enters too slowly (12mo lookback) to catch the 2023-24 recovery -> OOS Sharpe -0.591 ~= risk_based baseline -0.592. Huge decay (1.057) is regime-driven (bull IS, bear+recovery OOS). CONCLUSION: on a 12-name book the lever that matters OOS is re-entry speed of selection, not sizing. Next: faster re-entry signal. Single split; sizing reverted to risk_based default.

hypothesis: A faster momentum lookback (roc 6mo vs 12-1) re-enters quicker after the 2022 crash and lifts OOS on the watchlist
notes: REJECT. roc 6mo did WORSE than momentum_12_1+vol_target in BOTH windows (OOS -0.865 vs -0.591; IS -0.18 vs +0.466) AND deployed less (IS exp 0.53 vs 0.80). Faster lookback = more whipsaw, not better re-entry. THIRD straight watchlist rejection. Meta-pattern: momentum selection structurally under-deploys/whipsaws on a 12-name book (cf study Finding 1: universe dominates signal). Pivot away from watchlist-selection tweaks.

hypothesis: The vol-target overlay (regime gate + vol targeting) beats plain 12-1 momentum OOS on the diversified universe where momentum actually deploys
notes: FIRST NON-REJECT. vol_target_momentum beats momentum_12_1 OOS +0.278 vs -1.321 on diversified (vol_target sizing). Repeatable: the legacy risk_based diversified run also showed the overlay winning (+0.32 vs -0.63). BUT gate=TACTICAL not satellite: OOS +0.278 is BELOW the ~0.3-0.4 deflated-Sharpe noise threshold for ~100+ trials (fails DSR), and it does not beat buy-hold (SPY 0.53/QQQ 0.68). Single split. Next: multi-split + formal deflated Sharpe to see if it earns satellite.

hypothesis: Multi-split check: is the vol-target overlay edge on diversified robust across split boundaries, or regime-luck?
notes: REJECT for satellite; DOWNGRADES iter-4 tactical. Multi-split reveals regime-dependence: on the 2021-06-30 split the overlay BEAT baseline OOS (+0.278 vs -1.321, OOS contains 2022 crash) but on the 2022-06-30 split it LOSES to baseline (+0.359 vs +0.608, OOS is the recovery). The overlay is a CRASH HEDGE not robust alpha: its regime gate wins when OOS includes a drawdown it sidesteps, loses when OOS is a recovery it sits out. Confirms the deflated-Sharpe caution (iter-4 +0.278 was noise-band). The multi-split gate caught regime-luck a single split would have promoted.

hypothesis: A broad 200-name cross-section fixes the under-deployment that crippled momentum on 12 names and lets it perform
notes: META-FINDING CONFIRMED but OOS REJECT. Breadth fixed deployment: IS exposure 0.15(watchlist)->1.07(full), IS CAGR +27.7%, IS Sharpe 0.75 (QQQ-like) — momentum WORKS in-sample when it has a real cross-section. BUT OOS Sharpe -0.322 (decay 1.072) on the 2021-06-30 crash split, AND big CAVEAT: universe built from CURRENTLY-liquid names = survivorship bias (excludes delisted) -> IS is research-grade/inflated (data rule). So breadth is NECESSARY (fixes deployment) but not SUFFICIENT (OOS regime decay + survivorship). Single split. Next: multi-split on broad to see if OOS failure is just the 2022-crash split (cf iter-5).

hypothesis: Multi-split: is broad-universe momentum OOS failure (iter-6) general, or just the 2022-crash split?
notes: DIAGNOSIS: broad momentum is CRASH-VULNERABLE, not generally broken. Two-split: 2021-06-30 OOS -0.322 (window INCLUDES 2022 crash), 2022-06-30 OOS +0.932 (window is the RECOVERY, BEATS QQQ 0.68). Classic momentum-crash risk. Avg OOS ~0.305 sits at the deflated-Sharpe noise band. KEY: breadth fixes deployment (iter-6) and the vol-target overlay (iter-4/5) is a CRASH HEDGE -> the synthesis is overlay ON the broad universe: deploy fully via breadth, protect the 2022 crash via the overlay. That is the next experiment (iter-8). Survivorship caveat still applies.

hypothesis: SYNTHESIS: the vol-target overlay ON the broad universe protects the 2022 momentum crash that broke plain broad momentum (iter-6/7)
notes: SYNTHESIS WORKS on the crash split. Overlay OOS +0.209 vs baseline -0.322 (+0.53 swing) and decay 1.072->0.264. Breadth fixes deployment + overlay hedges the crash, as predicted across Findings 6-10. Best result of the loop so far. PENDING: recovery split (2022-06-30) where baseline broad scored +0.932 -- need to confirm the overlay does not give up the recovery before any satellite promotion. Gate tactical until split-robust. Survivorship caveat still applies.

hypothesis: Does the broad-universe overlay give up the recovery split, or is it split-robust?
notes: CAPSTONE of the price-momentum thread. Full 2-split on broad: overlay OOS {crash +0.209, recovery +0.336} vs baseline {-0.322, +0.932}. Overlay is the MOST ROBUST config found -- positive in BOTH regimes, near-zero decay (0.26/0.08) -- but NOT satellite: it loses the recovery split on raw Sharpe (trades upside for crash protection = a hedge). Mean OOS +0.273 is BELOW the ~0.3-0.4 deflated-Sharpe noise band and does not beat QQQ 0.68. CONCLUSION: on price-only + survivorship-biased data, momentum tops out at the noise band; the robust overlay is a legit lower-variance crash hedge but not buy-hold-beating alpha. CEILING CONFIRMED. Next lever is NOT another price permutation -- it is exiting degraded mode (fundamentals/sentiment). Pausing the price-momentum loop here.

hypothesis: Does the vol-target overlay edge survive the survivorship/look-ahead correction, or was it inflated like the momentum baseline (Finding 14)?
notes: OVERLAY SURVIVES DE-BIASING (encouraging). On the point-in-time universe, crash split: overlay OOS +0.51 vs baseline -0.216 (+0.73). Unlike the baseline whose IS collapsed 0.75->0.18 under de-biasing (Finding 14), the overlay edge STRENGTHENED (+0.51 vs look-ahead +0.209). Mechanism: baseline inflation was name-selection look-ahead; the overlay edge is REGIME TIMING (cut exposure in the 2022 crash), independent of universe membership -> robust to the correction. First result that gets STRONGER under rigorous correction. PENDING: PIT recovery split (2022-06-30) to check split-robustness + buy-hold comparison + deflated Sharpe before any promotion. Coverage survivorship (delisted absent) still applies.

hypothesis: Does the de-biased overlay edge hold on the recovery split too (split-robust), or give up upside as on the look-ahead universe?
notes: FIRST SATELLITE PROMOTION. Full de-biased 2-split: overlay beats baseline on BOTH splits -- crash +0.51 vs -0.216, recovery +0.475 vs +0.422. Mean OOS +0.49 vs baseline +0.10, both positive, negative decay (OOS>IS). OPPOSITE of the look-ahead universe (iter-9: overlay LOST recovery +0.34 vs +0.93) -- the look-ahead high-flyers had inflated the baseline recovery and HID the overlay edge. Survivorship correction revealed a true positive. GATE=SATELLITE (beats baseline all splits, low decay, survives de-biasing). NOT CORE: mean 0.49 < SPY buy-hold 0.53, does not beat buy-hold. CAVEATS: deflated-Sharpe check only marginal (both splits ~0.48-0.51 vs ~0.4 noise band for ~100 trials) -- a formal DSR is the next rigor step; coverage survivorship (delisted absent) remains.

hypothesis: Full-period reality check: does the satellite overlay beat SPY buy-hold on return AND drawdown?
notes: CONFIRMS satellite + finds a deadlock bug + leverage caveat. (1) DEADLOCK: naive full-period run gave CAGR 2.4%/Sharpe -0.095 because the 15% circuit breaker latched after the 2018 drawdown and a cashed book cant recover -> permanent halt (DOCUMENTED in main.py: go-to-cash timing strategies must set use_circuit_breaker:false). Walk-forward OOS windows started fresh post-2018 so never tripped it -> the satellite OOS Sharpes were valid. (2) CORRECTED full-period (cb off): CAGR 20.48%, Sharpe 0.572, MaxDD 41.6%, mean exp 1.07 (2022 exp 0.11 = gate cut the crash). Reconciles with OOS Sharpes 0.48-0.51 -> SATELLITE CONFIRMED, beats SPY (0.53) and matches QQQ return. (3) NOT CORE: MaxDD 41.6% WORSE than SPY 32.5% (not less-drawdown) and uses LEVERAGE (exp to 1.66) -> gross-exposure control needed before trust.

hypothesis: Does the satellite overlay edge survive removing the unintended leverage (rebalance_holds:true)?
notes: SATELLITE REVOKED -- the edge was leverage. Fixed the rebalancing gap (Finding 18): rebalance_holds:true re-sizes HELD positions each period -> exposure controlled (max 0.72 vs 2.0). De-levered full-period: CAGR 8.0% (was 20.5%), Sharpe 0.297 (was 0.572), MaxDD 21.1% (was 41.6%). Now BELOW SPY on return (8 vs 14.1) AND Sharpe (0.297 vs 0.53), back in the deflated-Sharpe noise band -> NOT alpha. Only virtue: MaxDD 21.1% < SPY 32.5% (crash hedge cuts drawdown) = a DEFENSIVE profile, not market-beating. DOWNGRADE satellite->reject. META: on price-only, survivorship-corrected, unleveraged, properly-rebalanced data, no momentum variant beats SPY risk-adjusted. The apparent winner was leverage. Real lever remains new information (fundamentals), data-blocked.

hypothesis: Does the low-volatility anomaly (a different, well-evidenced factor) beat SPY risk-adjusted on the rigorous de-biased basis?
notes: REJECT. low_volatility de-biased/unleveraged: CAGR 5.3%, Sharpe 0.157, MaxDD 10.9%. Below SPY on return AND Sharpe (0.157 vs 0.53); very low drawdown but too much return cost. The low-vol anomaly does NOT produce a Sharpe edge here. THIRD distinct family (momentum, vol-overlay, low-vol) to fail vs SPY risk-adjusted on rigorous price-only data. CONCLUSIVE: price-only investigation closed. Defensive factors cut drawdown but Sharpe stays below SPY; momentum under-deploys/whipsaws. Nothing beats indexing risk-adjusted with price alone. Remaining lever (fundamentals) is data-blocked -> human paid-data decision.
